description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, granular surface and a natural, earthy coloration. The stone features a prominent, rounded protrusion on one side and a flatter, broader base, suggesting it could serve as a low barrier or obstacle in an urban setting. Its surface is pitted and uneven, with subtle color variations ranging from light beige to brown, and some visible weathering marks. One side displays faint, hand-written markings, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone's overall form is asymmetrical, with a tapering end and a more bulbous, squat section on the opposite side. Its texture and coloration indicate it has been exposed to the elements, giving it a timeworn, authentic appearance.
description: A rough, irregularly shaped beige-brown stone with a rounded protrusion and a pitted, weathered surface. It features subtle color variations and visible cataloging marks on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, rounded protrusion and the granular, uneven surface texture.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone appears more elongated and narrow, emphasizing its tapering end and vertical ridges.
description_view_2: This side view shows the flat base and the hand-written cataloging marks, with the stone's squat, blocky profile visible.
description_view_3: The stone is seen from its narrowest angle, accentuating the taper and the subtle surface ridges running lengthwise.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or low barrier along pathways
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with no artificial carvings; subtle natural color gradations.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of surface erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil and vegetation, providing a subtle contrast to paved or manicured urban features.
